topic.what: Characterizing and benchmarking logging code security issues with LLMs
topic.how: Derived taxonomy of 4 categories/10 patterns, built SecLogging benchmark (101 issues), evaluated 8 LLMs on detection/repair capabilities
topic.why_matters: Insecure logging can leak sensitive data or enable log injection attacks; LLMs show uneven effectiveness at detecting/repairing these issues, revealing need for better prompting strategies and model improvements

characteristics.what_it_measures: LLM accuracy in detecting security issue categories in logging code
characteristics.vs_baseline: No baseline comparison; absolute metrics reported
characteristics.evidence: Table in Section 5.2: category-level accuracy ranges across 8 models and 4 settings
characteristics.context: Range spans GPT-4.1-nano (lowest) to DeepSeek-V3 with +D (highest)

characteristics.what_it_measures: LLM accuracy in detecting specific security patterns
characteristics.evidence: Section 5.2: concrete patterns like IL-At, SS-Cr achieve >70%; abstract patterns like RM-Ms fall below 30%
characteristics.context: Concrete token-based issues easier than abstract logic-based issues like missing redaction

characteristics.what_it_measures: Textual similarity between LLM-generated patches and ground truth repairs
characteristics.evidence: Table 3: Patch Similarity averages range from 18.8% (DeepSeek-R1-32b +D) to 69.3% (DeepSeek-V3 Base)
characteristics.context: DeepSeek-V3 best performer; Base setting outperforms enriched contexts for repair

characteristics.what_it_measures: Improvement in detection accuracy when adding issue description
characteristics.evidence: DeepSeek-V3 average accuracy increases from 41.6% to 52.5% with +D setting
characteristics.context: Issue description (+D) provides more consistent improvement than pattern explanation (+E)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance decline when combining explanation and description
characteristics.evidence: Section 5.2: combined +E+D often degrades performance compared to +D alone
characteristics.context: Excessive context introduces noise that overwhelms models

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of LLM-generated repairs deemed effective by human evaluation
characteristics.evidence: Section 5.3: DeepSeek-V3 achieves 83.2% effective repairs; DeepSeek-R1-32b achieves 75%
characteristics.context: Manual evaluation of DeepSeek-V3 (Base) and DeepSeek-R1-32b (+D) cases

novelty: First taxonomy of logging code security issues (4 categories, 10 patterns)
First benchmark dataset SecLogging with 101 real-world logging security issues
Systematic evaluation of 8 LLMs on detection and repair of logging security issues
Finding that issue description alone improves detection more than combined context
open_problems: Improving LLM detection of abstract logic-based issues (RM patterns)
Enhancing LLM repair quality without introducing complexity
Expanding benchmark to cover more languages and logging frameworks
